Narrative: A stout bee in a stained leaf-satchel hovers over a bark table. She's surrounded by pollen puffs and crystallized nectar, currently mid-chew on a piece of sticky honeycomb.\n\nShe eyes you with deep suspicion, hugging a jar of jelly to her chest. "Want a bite? Fine," she hums with a lazy, raspy tune. "But I'm only parting with one—my stomach can't handle any more heartbreak. Leave your coin and be gone. "

A tall merchant wasp flies low, hovering just inches above the dead grass. He is dragging a heavy, shimmering a [url=card]{card:dt_card}[/url] card behind him with his rear legs—the very last item in his inventory.\n\nThe drone of his wings is jagged and metallic as he struggles with the weight, his back turned to you. He hasn't noticed your presence yet, preoccupied with dragging his haul toward the darker edge of the garden.

A huge spider with legs like silver needles sits amidst a web of shimmering scrolls. She is busy binding cards with glowing silk.\n\nShe stops. Her eyes fix on you, cold and empty. "Threads," she vibrates. "Cards... packs..."
